stance: EVIDENCE-SPLIT HYBRID with a hard, predictive criterion applied per family. EXTEND THE LANGUAGE only when (1) the semantic capability is reused by >=3 catalog forms, (2) its client-side obligation is decidable syntax-directed with compile-time constants only (no inference, no invariant synthesis beyond the existing requires prologue), and (3) its absence would force per-instance trusted code rather than a per-mechanism rule. SEAL AS BUILTIN-TCB when par depends on internals whose safety argument is either a partial-initialization invariant no frontend-simple checker can verify (vec spare capacity, SwissTable slot/control coupling, B-tree nodes, slot recycling) or a paper-grade concurrency proof (memory orderings, deferred reclamation, Chase-Lev fences) — i.e., exactly the implement-rarely/use-often structures where one hostile review + model checking amortizes over all users, and where the sealed interface leaves zero writer-suppliable trust. REJECT (existing pattern/composition) when the demand map's own substitutability analysis shows a taught composition of catalog forms reaches par (graphs, trees, LRU, interning, timer wheel, sharded maps, intrusive links). The criterion is falsifiable: if a checked-source form built on a claimed language delta misses its preregistered band, the delta was wrong and the form moves to the sealed side (preregistered for codecs in experiment 3).
summary: The catalog is n=22 taught forms plus 8 taught composition patterns, split by the criterion into three tiers. Tier A (language-primitive, 6 deltas that each serve >=3 forms): branded region indices id<'r,T> (check-free arena/graph/tree/interner deref), margin byte buffers (codec wild copies, cursor amortized bounds, SIMD tails), declarative layout schemas instantiating validated zero-copy views, a portable SIMD value core with named intrinsics and one-time ISA dispatch, a machine scalar kit (endian unaligned load/store, bit ops, guaranteed-lowering select, prefetch/NT, alignment), and a minimal concurrency core (region-scoped spawn, structural share judgment, linear channel endpoints, DRF-by-construction — writers never see a memory ordering). Tier B (builtin-TCB, sealed internals behind fully checked interfaces): the sequence family vec/deque with inline-capacity knob, SwissTable map and B-tree omap, generational pool, mutex/once, SPSC+MPMC channels, snapshot cell + atomic counter/flag, scoped-thread + work-stealing parallel runtime, buffered/direct file I/O, completion I/O ring with linearly-loaned buffers, read-only filemap, OS facade, and the runtime allocator — roughly 15k LOC of ledgered trusted kernels verified by hostile review, exhaustive small-bound model checking, and differential fuzzing against reference oracles (hashbrown, slotmap, crossbeam, liburing). Tier C (checked-library, fully inside the safety proof): sort/search, hash/checksum kernels, formatting/builder kernels, byte cursors, WAL/atomic-replace recipes, and all SIMD scan/classify kernels — these ride the measured requires-prologue elision (27/27 bounds discharged, 1.71x) so every check stays on. Estimated kernel growth: ~47 rules (90 -> ~137), with sealed-form op signatures living in the taught catalog, not the kernel. Coverage of the 51-scenario map: 44 PAR, 1 WIN (checked-law deterministic parallel reduction, backed by the measured 3.3x reassociation result), 6 honest PARTIAL (papaya-class read-mostly concurrent maps, unproven codec parity, shared-mutable mmap/IPC, inbound FFI callbacks), 0 silent gaps.

forms.name: vec<T, INLINE=0> (sequence family root)
forms.contract: Owns a contiguous growable run of any owned T. push moves T in (amortized O(1), geometric growth); pop moves T out as option<T>; index read/write borrows (OOB traps deterministically — one spelling); reserve/truncate; as_view yields slice<'r,T> region-tied borrow so all algorithms are written against views; spare-capacity loan op fill_via(sink) lets sealed I/O fill the uninitialized tail and advance len by the count actually written; bulk copy/fill ops with an NT-store flag. INLINE=N keeps storage in the owner footprint until len>N, then spills transparently (sealed). Growth OOM traps; try_reserve is the fallible spelling.
forms.perf_mechanism: Sealed (ptr,len,cap) kernel preserves the exact Vec machine shape: tail-line-only appends, one predicted capacity branch, memcpy growth at DRAM bandwidth, stride-1 prefetched iteration; the partial-initialization invariant ([0,len) init, [len,cap) uninit) lives in ~300 LOC of TCB because no frontend-simple checker can carry it; INLINE knob eliminates the malloc/free pair for SmallVector workloads (3-5x on 2-8 element lists).
forms.source_shape: let mut v: vec<u64> = vec.new(); v.reserve(n); loop { v.push(x); }; let s: slice<'r,u64> = v.as_view(); ... let ops: vec<Op, 4> = vec.new(); // inline until 5th push

forms.name: deque<T> (ring sibling of vec)
forms.contract: Same ownership contract as vec plus push_front/pop_front; as_views yields the two contiguous slice views; grows on demand; single-threaded, no synchronization. OOB traps; pops return option<T>.
forms.perf_mechanism: Power-of-two masked head/tail indices over the same sealed storage kernel: push/pop = one masked index update + one move, VecDeque-class cache behavior, growth = one bulk memcpy; shares the vec TCB so it costs ~2 extra op rules, not a new kernel.
forms.source_shape: let mut q: deque<Node> = deque.new(); q.push_back(n); match q.pop_front() { some(n) => ..., none => break }

forms.name: map<K,V, INLINE=0> (SwissTable)
forms.contract: Owns K,V. insert moves both in, returns option<V> (displaced); find -> option<&V>, find_uniq -> option<&uniq V>; remove -> option<V>; entry API without closures: entry(k) returns enum { occupied(&uniq V), vacant(tok) } where tok is an affine region-tied vacancy token consumed by insert_at(tok, v) — get-or-insert in one hash + one probe; find_by(env, keyview) probes by a borrowed byte/key view via an env-struct comparator (no allocation, enables interning); hasher is an env-struct contract instance (default = checked foldhash-class). Growth OOM traps; INLINE=N is a linear-scan inline array until spill (SsoHashMap shape).
forms.perf_mechanism: Sealed internals keep the full SwissTable mechanism: separate control-byte array, 7-bit fragments, 16-byte SIMD group probe (~2 cache lines per hit at any size), 87.5% load factor, group tombstone elision; the control-byte/slot init coupling is unverifiable frontend-simply, hence TCB (~2k LOC); the affine vacancy token preserves the one-probe entry idiom without closures.
forms.source_shape: match m.entry(k) { occupied(v) => { v.count = add(v.count, 1); }, vacant(t) => { m.insert_at(t, new_counter()); } }

forms.name: omap<K,V> (B-tree ordered map)
forms.contract: Ordered-key map: insert/find/remove plus range(lo,hi) -> in-order region-tied cursor, first/last, pred/succ. Same ownership and trap behavior as map. Point users are taught to use map; omap exists only for the range contract.
forms.perf_mechanism: Sealed B-tree with fanout ~11-16, ~256B nodes, branchless intra-node scan: ~5-8 line touches per lookup at 1M keys and near-streaming leaf walks for ranges — the absl::btree/BTreeMap machine shape; node partial-init invariants are why it is TCB (~1.5k LOC) rather than checked source.
forms.source_shape: let mut r = t.range(lo, hi); loop { match r.next() { some(kv) => ..., none => break } }

forms.name: pool<T> (generational slab)
forms.contract: insert(T) -> handle<T> (8-byte copy struct: index+generation); get(h) -> option<&T> and get_uniq(h) -> option<&uniq T> return none on stale generation — never trap, never alias freed data; remove(h) -> option<T> moves the value out and recycles the slot O(1) WITHOUT re-zeroing; dense iteration over live slots. Handles are plain data: storable in structs, sendable, and the blessed substitute for pointer graphs.
forms.perf_mechanism: Sealed slot array with intrusive in-place freelist (union of T and next-free) and per-slot generation: insert/remove ~2-5 ns with zero allocator traffic, get = bounds+generation compare+indexed load (1 line), iteration near vec speed; slot reuse is type-changing storage — exactly the well-typed-UAF shape the checker rightly rejects in source — so it is TCB (~200 LOC) with dynamically-detected staleness keeping T1 intact.
forms.source_shape: copy struct Conn { fd: i32, state: u8 } ... let h: handle<Sess> = pool.insert(sess); match pool.get_uniq(h) { some(s) => { s.state = 2; }, none => { /* stale: connection already closed */ } }

forms.name: arena<'r,T> + id<'r,T> (region arena with branded indices)
forms.contract: Existing lexical-region arena extended so alloc(v) -> id<'r,T>: a copy, storable, nonforgeable index branded by the arena's region. arena[id] dereferences with ZERO dynamic checks — the brand proves the id was minted by this arena and the arena is append-only while 'r lives. Whole-region mass free at scope end; drop-carrying T runs compiler-derived drops at region exit; reset-and-reuse via the existing region staircase pattern (fresh 'r each cycle statically invalidates all old ids).
forms.perf_mechanism: Allocation stays bump-class (~1-2 ns) and deref becomes base + id*stride with no bounds or generation check — matching C pointer graphs and strictly beating safe Rust's checked index arenas; because ids are data, parent pointers and cross-links are plain fields, giving allocation-order locality for tree/IR traversal (the bumpalo/rustc-arena win) fully inside the safety proof.
forms.source_shape: region 'a { let ar: arena<'a, Expr> = arena.new(); let lhs: id<'a,Expr> = ar.alloc(e1); let e: id<'a,Expr> = ar.alloc(Expr.add { lhs: lhs, rhs: rhs }); let n: &Expr = &ar[e]; }

forms.name: layout views (declarative zero-copy overlays)
forms.contract: Writer declares a layout schema: field name, scalar type, endianness, byte offset, total size, alignment; the compiler instantiates validate(bytes<'r>) -> option<view<'r,L>> (one length/shape/invariant check) and typed field accessors on the view. Each field read after validation is guaranteed to compile to exactly one load (+one bswap for foreign endianness), zero residual checks; uniq views permit field writes. View lifetime is region-tied to the buffer. Malformed input yields none at validate, never a fault later.
forms.perf_mechanism: Moves the entire checking cost to one amortized validation, exactly the zerocopy/capnp validate-then-view discipline, but machine-generated from a declarative schema instead of trusted derive code — codegen is bit-identical to a C packed-struct dereference (asm-diff gate), covering packets, DB page headers, records, and mmap'd formats.
forms.source_shape: layout Ipv4 { ver_ihl: u8 @0; total_len: u16be @2; ttl: u8 @8; src: u32be @12; size 20; align 1 } ... match Ipv4.validate(pkt) { some(v) => { let len: u16 = v.total_len; ... }, none => drop_packet() }

forms.name: byte cursor over margin buffers
forms.contract: bytes<'r, margin M> is a byte view refinement guaranteeing M writable/readable slop bytes past the logical length (created by vec/allocator slop or validated framing; margin bytes are never observable as initialized data). The cursor library provides take_u8/u16le/u32be/u64le/varint/skip with one compile-time-elidable bounds check per field (not per byte); exhaustion returns an error value at the boundary, never a trap mid-parse; decoded string/byte fields are zero-copy sub-views.
forms.perf_mechanism: Varint decode = one unaligned 8-byte load + shift/mask (kills the byte-loop mispredict chain); margin makes the wide load at the tail legal without a slow path; requires-prologue facts hoist per-field checks to one per message — the upb/prost machine shape, prost-class GB/s, fully checked source.
forms.source_shape: let mut c = cursor.new(msg); let tag: u32 = c.varint32()?; let len: u32 = c.varint32()?; let body: bytes<'r> = c.take(len)?;

forms.name: SIMD core (portable vectors + named intrinsics + dispatch)
forms.contract: Width-explicit vector value types (u8x16/u8x32/u16x16/u32x8/u64x4/f32x8...) with lane ops, shuffle (pshufb-as-table), compare-to-mask, movemask-to-scalar, masked load/store legal only within a margin or proven bounds; a closed named-intrinsic set (clmul, crc32step, aes-round, vpermb) behind the same types with per-ISA availability; a dispatch attribute compiles a function per ISA (SSE2/AVX2/AVX-512/NEON) and selects once at startup. Pure value semantics: misuse is a type error or a slow kernel, never UB; slice loads carry the ordinary (elidable) bounds obligations.
forms.perf_mechanism: Preserves the highway/std::simd mechanism: 16-64 lanes per instruction, movemask+tzcnt bridging to scalar control in 2 instructions, one-time CPUID dispatch; this is the substrate that makes memchr-class, simdjson-stage-1-class, simdutf-class, and hash kernels writable as checked source instead of trusted blobs.
forms.source_shape: dispatch(avx2, neon) fn find(h: bytes<'r, margin 32>, n: u8) -> option<u64> { loop { let v: u8x32 = load(h, i); let m: u32 = movemask(eq(v, splat(n))); match m { 0 => { i = add(i, 32); }, _ => { break some(add(i, tzcnt(m))); } } } }

forms.name: machine scalar kit
forms.contract: load_le/load_be/store_le/store_be of u16/u32/u64/u128 at arbitrary byte offsets in a byte view (one elidable bounds check, guaranteed single load/store + at most one bswap); integer methods popcount/tzcnt/lzcnt/rotl/rotr/pdep/pext each guaranteed one instruction (dispatch fallback on pre-BMI AMD); select(cond, a, b) with two tiers — perf tier (guaranteed cmov-class lowering unless the compiler proves the branch profitable) and strict tier (verified never-branch, for constant time); prefetch(place) with no-op semantics (never faults, never observable, exempt from borrow obligations); NT-store flag on bulk copy/fill; align(N) attribute on types and allocations; padded<T> 64-byte wrapper.
forms.perf_mechanism: These are the leaf mechanisms every SOTA kernel bottoms out in: single-instruction bit ops make SwissTable-probe/roaring/bitboard loops exact; select makes branchless partition and branchless lower_bound expressible with a lowering guarantee Rust does not give; padding kills false sharing (5-20x on contended counters); all are pure value ops with zero new aliasing surface.
forms.source_shape: let x: u64 = load_le_u64(buf, off); let lo: u64 = select(lt(probe, key), add(lo, half), lo); prefetch(&table[h1]);

forms.name: sort & search library
forms.contract: sort(slice, cmp_env) unstable (ipnsort shape, O(n log n) worst-case guaranteed, near-O(n) on sorted input) and sort_stable(slice, cmp_env) (driftsort shape) — stability is the only flag, one spelling each; internal radix specialization for primitive keys; lower_bound/binary_search/partition_point branchless over sorted slices; no allocation in search, bounded scratch in stable sort. Comparator is an env-struct contract instance.
forms.perf_mechanism: Branchless partition = unconditional store + select-predicated pointer advance (the pdqsort trick) written directly against the select primitive; insertion sort at small n stays in L1; all index arithmetic is slice-local so requires facts discharge the bounds checks — checked source at ipnsort-class throughput, no TCB.
forms.source_shape: sort(v.as_view_uniq(), ByKey { key: extract_ts }); let i: u64 = lower_bound(s, k);

forms.name: hash & checksum kernels
forms.contract: Env-struct hasher instances over byte views: foldhash-class (default map hasher), siphash (keyed, DoS-resistant), xxh3-class bulk, crc32c (hardware, 3 interleaved streams), one-shot and streaming; seeds from the OS facade entropy. Pure functions of their input; no failure modes.
forms.perf_mechanism: Wide unaligned loads + widening multiplies / clmul / crc32step from the machine kit and SIMD core run entirely in registers: ~4-6 ns 16B one-shot, 15-50 GB/s bulk — the xxh3/isa-l machine shape as checked source, since hashing has no ownership surface at all.
forms.source_shape: let h: u64 = foldhash.hash(seed, key_bytes); let c: u32 = crc32c.frame(page_view);

forms.name: byte builder & numeric formatting
forms.contract: A write-protocol contract (env-struct sink over vec<u8>) that third-party formatters target; append bytes/ASCII/str at memcpy speed into reserved tail; itoa-class integer emission and ryu-class shortest-float emission from const lookup tables; final move-out of the vec as the finished buffer. Growth traps on OOM like vec.
forms.perf_mechanism: u64->decimal via digit-pair tables is ~4 ns vs ~100 ns snprintf (no locale, no format parse); all table indices are provably in-range so checks elide; appends are bounds-elided memcpy into reserved capacity — the String+itoa/ryu shape with zero trusted code.
forms.source_shape: let mut b: vec<u8> = vec.new(); b.reserve(256); append_str(&uniq b, "id="); append_u64(&uniq b, id); let out: vec<u8> = b;

forms.name: scoped threads + parallel runtime (parallel-for / fork-join)
forms.contract: scope 'p { spawn(s, env) } spawns threads whose env is either moved in or borrows 'p-region data; all threads provably join before 'p ends (same lexical discipline as arena drop); join returns the worker's value or propagates its trap to the boundary. parallel_for(slice, body_env) splits recursively and mutates provably disjoint chunks (split_at consumes the uniq view, so disjointness is by construction); parallel_reduce(slice, monoid_env) requires the checked assoc/identity laws and is deterministic when asked. Per-worker mutable state is an env struct handed to each worker (replaces TLS for writers). The sealed interior is a Chase-Lev work-stealing deque, also exposed as wsdeque owner-push/pop/steal ops for runtime authors.
forms.perf_mechanism: Owner-side deque ops have no atomic RMW (release store; one SeqCst fence on pop; CAS only on the last-element race) so task overhead is rayon-class ~20-50 ns; geometric splitting makes steals logarithmically rare and bandwidth the ceiling; the fence pairing is a paper-grade proof done once in TCB with model checking — exactly the implement-rarely case; checked reassociation laws additionally license vectorized/reassociated reductions C and Rust compilers cannot legally do on floats/overflow-checked ints (measured 3.3x class).
forms.source_shape: scope 'p { parallel_for(data.as_view_uniq(), 4096, Body { lut: &lut }); } let s: u64 = parallel_reduce(v.as_view(), SumMonoid {});

forms.name: mutex<T> / once<T> / condvar
forms.contract: mutex<T> owns the protected data (lock-protects-data, not a bare lock); lock() returns an affine region-tied guard granting &uniq T, released at scope end; adaptive futex parking (bounded spin then wait), no busy burn; condvar wait(guard, pred_env); once<T> race-safe one-shot init then &T at a load+predicted-branch. A trap while holding is a deterministic process-boundary trap (traps-to-the-boundary; no poisoning state).
forms.perf_mechanism: Word-sized lock inline with the data keeps lock word + protected state on one line: ~10-20 ns uncontended lock/unlock (two RMWs on an owned line, zero syscalls), parking_lot/absl shape; the guard-as-affine-borrow makes leaking or double-unlocking unrepresentable without any checker addition beyond existing move rules.
forms.source_shape: let g = m.lock(); g.data.hits = add(g.data.hits, 1); /* guard drops at scope end */

forms.name: channels: spsc<T> ring + mpmc<T> bounded
forms.contract: spsc.new(cap) -> (tx, rx): two AFFINE endpoints — single-producer/single-consumer is compile-time fact, not runtime hope; push/pop are wait-free, allocation-free, fail with full/empty result values (backpressure is the API); batched reserve/commit for slot batches. mpmc.new(cap) -> cloneable endpoints, Vyukov slot-sequence internals, ownership of T moves through the queue; blocking recv integrates futex parking. Values crossing must satisfy the share/send judgment.
forms.perf_mechanism: Because endpoint uniqueness is a linear-type fact, the SPSC form keeps the zero-RMW single-writer-per-counter mechanism (padded counters, cached opposing index): ~8-15 ns/round-trip, >100M items/s batched — the exact win MPMC must give up, and the unification-by-linearity the demand map wished for; MPMC keeps Vyukov's one-RMW-per-op with slot-local publication (~15-25 ns uncontended).
forms.source_shape: let (tx, rx) = spsc.new::<Frame>(1024); scope 'p { spawn(s, Producer { tx: tx }); spawn(s, Consumer { rx: rx }); }

forms.name: shared<T> snapshot cell + counter/flag atomic cells
forms.contract: shared<T>: publish(v) moves a new value in and retires the old via internal epoch reclamation; read() returns a guard-scoped &T snapshot (pin ~2 ns, readers write no shared lines). counter: relaxed fetch_add/load for statistics. flag: release set / acquire check, the shutdown and once idiom. This closed trio plus channels/mutex IS the writer-facing atomics vocabulary — there is no ordering parameter anywhere in source.
forms.perf_mechanism: Covers the three stereotyped idioms that are ~95% of real atomic use at hardware par (fixed per-form orderings chosen once, so ARM pays no blanket SeqCst tax); snapshot reads scale linearly with cores (arc-swap/RCU shape, ~2 ns/read) where RwLock-class reader counts plateau at 2-3 threads; all epoch machinery is invisible TCB.
forms.source_shape: let cfg: &Routes = routes.read(&pin); ... routes.publish(new_table); stats.requests.add(1); match shutdown.check() { true => break, false => continue }

forms.name: file I/O: buffered streams + direct mode + durability ops
forms.contract: open/read/write over a 64-256KB internal buffer; fill_buf() -> region-tied &[u8] borrow of the internal buffer + consume(n) for zero-copy in-place parsing; read_into(vec) fills spare capacity via the uninit-extent loan (no memset ever); explicit flush; O_DIRECT mode requiring align(4096) buffers for database engines; low-level durability ops: fdatasync, fsync_dir, atomic rename. Errors are result values with OS error codes; no failure is silent.
forms.perf_mechanism: Amortizes the ~300-600 ns syscall to ~0.01 ns/byte; borrow-the-buffer removes the second copy (ripgrep-class >2 GB/s scanning); direct mode preserves the RocksDB/SQLite discipline; the zero-memset read path is a codegen-gated guarantee, matching C exactly.
forms.source_shape: let b: &[u8] = r.fill_buf()?; let n: u64 = parse_records(b); r.consume(n); w.write(frame)?; f.fdatasync()?;

forms.name: completion I/O ring + sockets
forms.contract: ring.submit(op { fd, buf, kind }) MOVES the buffer (a vec<u8>) into the in-flight op and returns an affine completion token; completions() yields (token, result, buffer moved back) — the buffer is linearly loaned to the kernel and untouchable while in flight, by the existing affine rules; multishot accept/recv, vectored writes, integrated timers and cancellation; lowers to io_uring where available, epoll/kqueue + nonblocking emulation elsewhere. Socket options (NODELAY, REUSEPORT) exposed as declarative flags.
forms.perf_mechanism: Syscalls-per-op approach 0 via batched submission and shared-memory completions (io_uring ~2x epoll on syscall-bound echo); because buffer loans are static ownership facts rather than runtime refcounts, registered fixed buffers are usable by default — the exact lifetime problem that forces Arc-based buffer management in Rust io_uring wrappers disappears; steady state is allocation-free via buffer recycling.
forms.source_shape: let t = ring.submit(op.recv { fd: c, buf: buf })?; ... match ring.next_completion() { (t, ok(n), b) => { handle(b.as_view_len(n)); ring.submit(op.send { fd: c, buf: b })?; } }

forms.name: filemap (read-only memory map)
forms.contract: map_ro(path) -> filemap owning the mapping; view() -> region-tied frozen byte view for layout views and cursors; madvise hints as declarative flags; external truncation SIGBUS is converted to a defined deterministic trap (with an optional advisory-lock discipline to prevent it). Shared-mutable MAP_SHARED IPC is explicitly NOT in this form (deferred; see risks).
forms.perf_mechanism: Warm access is pure page-cache loads — zero syscalls, zero copies (LMDB-class ~100-200 ns warm gets when combined with layout views); hugepage/advice flags preserve the tuning surface; immutability of the mapped file is the trust boundary the form states honestly rather than hides.
forms.source_shape: let m = filemap.map_ro(path)?; let idx: bytes<'m> = m.view(); match PageHdr.validate(slice(idx, off, 4096)) { some(p) => ..., none => err }

forms.name: extern-C boundary records
forms.contract: An extern declaration names a C function with its ABI signature, repr(C) layouts, and a stated ownership/aliasing/lifetime contract per parameter (in/out/borrowed, pointer+len pairs from views, who frees); declarations live only in toolchain-gated, human-approved trusted record files — ordinary source cannot introduce them (no writer-suppliable trust); calls compile to a bare ABI call. Out-calls only (D4); whitefoot-called-from-C and callback in-calls are deferred.
forms.perf_mechanism: The machine shape is identical to a C-to-C call (~1-2 ns: ABI registers, no pinning, no stack switch, no marshaling) — cgo's ~40-60 ns is the explicit anti-target; buffers cross as pointer+len derived from views with zero copies because layout is bit-compatible by the repr(C) rule.
forms.source_shape: // in approved.trust: extern c fn BLAS_dgemm(a: cptr<f64> borrowed(len=an), ...) effects(io) ... // in source: BLAS_dgemm(av.as_cptr(), ...)

forms.name: OS facade (process/time/entropy/shutdown)
forms.contract: spawn_process (posix_spawn-backed: fds/env/cwd control, pipes, wait->status), monotonic and wall clocks (vDSO, hot-path safe), entropy (getrandom-seeded userspace CSPRNG buffer), and shutdown signals delivered as a flag/channel — signals are consumed, never handled in user code. All failures are result values.
forms.perf_mechanism: The differentiating choices are sealed implementation facts invisible to consumers: posix_spawn is ~60 us independent of parent RSS vs millisecond fork of large parents (ninja-class throughput), clock reads ~20-25 ns with no kernel entry, entropy ~2-5 ns per call from the buffer — the clearest n=1 facade in the map.
forms.source_shape: let t0: u64 = clock.monotonic_ns(); let child = proc.spawn(cmd)?; let st = child.wait()?;

forms.name: runtime allocator (not writer-facing)
forms.contract: The single global allocator behind box/vec/map/pool/arena chunks: alloc/free/realloc any size from any thread, cross-thread free, size-class + per-thread heaps; writers never call it directly and cannot replace it from ordinary source. Authoring a NEW allocator in checked whitefoot is out of scope by design (its internals are the canonical type-changing-reuse shape).
forms.perf_mechanism: mimalloc-class internals: TLS heap, size-class free-list push/pop ~10-20 instructions, zero atomics on the fast path, batched remote frees — ~15-25 ns alloc+free pair and larson-class scaling; sealing it lets every container above inherit SOTA allocation without any writer-visible unsafety.
forms.source_shape: (invisible: let b: box<T> = box.new(v); v.push(x); — all routed through it)

language_deltas: copy struct declaration: records whose fields are all scalars/copy types may be declared 'copy struct', making them Copy (bitwise duplicable, no drop); resolves the open records question explicitly and declaratively — one spelling, checker verifies field-wise copyability structurally. (~1 rule)

Branded region index id<'r,T>: arena alloc mints an id branded with the arena's region; ids are copy data, storable in structs; the deref form arena[id] typechecks only when the arena's region syntactically matches the brand, and is defined check-free because arenas are append-only while 'r lives and ids are nonforgeable (no arithmetic on ids, no construction from integers). (~3 rules)

Margin byte views bytes<'r, margin M>: a refinement type guaranteeing M extra allocated bytes past the logical length; ops may load/store up to a compile-time-constant M past len; margin contents are never observable as initialized program values (writes into margin are semantically discarded; reads feed only lane operations whose margin lanes are masked out of results). Created by allocation slop (vec reserve) or validated framing. (~3 rules)

Layout schemas + validated views: a declarative layout item (typed fields with endianness, constant offsets, size, align); a validation judgment producing view<'r,L> from a byte view; a field-access rule guaranteeing single-load lowering on validated views; uniq-view field writes; a handoff rule from views to cursors for variable-offset regions. (~6 rules)

SIMD value core: vector types as first-class values; lane-op, shuffle, compare-to-mask, movemask typing rules; masked load/store legal only under margin or discharged bounds; the closed named-intrinsic table with per-ISA availability; the dispatch attribute (per-ISA compilation, one startup selection, semantic equivalence obligation across variants). (~8 rules)

Machine scalar kit: endian-explicit unaligned load/store on byte views with single-instruction lowering guarantee; the fixed bit-op method table (popcount/tzcnt/lzcnt/rot/pdep/pext) with one-instruction guarantee and declared fallbacks; select(c,a,b) value form with perf and strict (verified branch-free) tiers; prefetch with vacuous semantics exempt from borrow/validity obligations; NT flag on bulk copy/fill; align(N) attribute and padded<T>. (~8 rules)

Concurrency core: region-scoped spawn (thread lifetime = lexical region, join-before-region-end reuses the existing region discipline); a structural share judgment computed like Copy (a type is sendable if it contains no borrows — already banned in data — and shareable-by-& if it transitively contains no sealed interior-mutable cell); linear endpoint rule (channel/ring endpoints are affine non-copy values); the DRF-by-construction theorem: programs cannot express unsynchronized shared mutation, so sealed-form publication contracts (stated per form) suffice and NO user-facing memory-ordering vocabulary exists; blocks effect-row label for parking ops. (~8 rules)

Uninit-extent loan: a vec spare-capacity loan op type (fill_via) whereby a sealed filler receives the uninitialized tail and the length advances only by the filler's returned count, with the TCB boundary (syscall/decoder kernel) trusted to have initialized exactly that prefix; checked-source writers never touch the loan — they use cursor/push ops. (~2 rules)

Sealed-form mechanism: opaque builtin type families with fixed op signatures, effect rows, and stated semantic contracts; no reflection into internals; op calls typecheck as ordinary function calls; the spec rule states what a sealed op may assume and must guarantee (the general mechanism is 3 rules; each form's op signatures live in the taught catalog appendix, not the kernel). (~3 rules)
io effect-row label: boundary operations carry io in their effect row so purity remains visible to the optimizer and to reviewers. (~1 rule)

Extern-C trusted records: extern declaration grammar with repr(C) layout rule, parameter ownership/lifetime contract vocabulary, bare-call typing, and the rule that such declarations are only admissible from toolchain-gated human-approved trusted record files. (~4 rules)

coverage.scenario: Growable contiguous vector (append-heavy owned buffer)
coverage.how: Sealed vec preserves the exact (ptr,len,cap) machine shape: tail-line append, one predicted branch, memcpy growth, stride-1 iteration; slice views make all algorithms container-independent; requires-prologue elision (measured 27/27 discharge class) keeps interior bounds checks free in loops. Experiment-class evidence (D9a first-green wins vs shipped Rust) says the shape is reachable by weak writers.

coverage.scenario: Small/inline vector (allocation-count-dominated short sequences)
coverage.how: INLINE=N knob on the same sealed vec (absl::InlinedVector shape): no heap while len<=N, sealed spill; eliminates the malloc/free pair and keeps elements on the owner's lines — the 3-5x LLVM-workload win with zero new catalog surface.

coverage.scenario: String/byte builder (append-only formatting and serialization buffer)
coverage.how: vec<u8> + the checked write-protocol and itoa/ryu-class table kernels: memcpy-tail appends, ~4 ns u64 emission, one reservation; snprintf-class is structurally impossible to write accidentally because the taught spelling IS the table kernel.

coverage.scenario: Sorting owned runs (comparison and key-based)
coverage.how: Checked-library ipnsort/driftsort shapes built on the select primitive (branchless partition = unconditional store + predicated advance), small-n insertion, run detection, internal radix path for primitive keys; all index math is slice-local so checks elide — no TCB needed.

coverage.scenario: Binary search / lookup over sorted contiguous runs
coverage.how: Branchless lower_bound via select (offset += (probe<key)*half) as checked library over slice views; Eytzinger is a taught layout transform over the same search with prefetch hints — covers the giant-frozen-table split without a new container.

coverage.scenario: General hash map/set (unordered point lookup, medium-to-large n)
coverage.how: Sealed SwissTable (control bytes, SIMD group probe, 87.5% load, tombstone elision) with checked foldhash-class default; entry via affine vacancy token keeps get-or-insert at one hash + one probe without closures; gated by experiment 1 (<=1.10x hashbrown).

coverage.scenario: Tiny map/set (n <= ~8-16, allocation- and hash-cost dominated)
coverage.how: INLINE knob on the same map: inline linear-scan pairs until spill (SsoHashMap/SmallDenseMap shape) — skips hashing and allocation at tiny n; a knob, not a form.

coverage.scenario: Ordered map with range queries (B-tree class)
coverage.how: Sealed B-tree omap with fanout ~11-16 and region-tied range cursors; genuinely a second associative form per the map's own analysis; RB-tree-class shapes are not in the catalog at all, so the fail shape is unwritable.

coverage.scenario: Bounded cache with eviction (LRU class)
coverage.how: Taught composition exactly as the map prescribes: map (handle values) + pool slab + embedded u32/handle recency links as plain copy fields; CLOCK/S3-FIFO are link-discipline variants of the same pattern. The required micro-capability (embedded index links) exists because handles are data.

coverage.scenario: Interning / deduplication (value -> stable small id)
coverage.how: Taught composition: arena byte store + map.find_by with an env-struct comparator resolving (offset,len) spans against the arena (borrowed-key probe, no allocation) + id vector; branded ids serve as symbols; the real requirement the map identified — borrowed-key lookup — is a map op, delivered.

coverage.scenario: Concurrent hash map (shared read-mostly or sharded-write lookup)
coverage.how: Sharded = checked composition (array<mutex<map>> + hash-bit shard pick) reaches dashmap-class for mixed loads; read-copy tables via shared<T> snapshot cell reach arc-swap-class linear read scaling. MISSING: papaya/evmap-class per-key-mutable read-mostly maps with linear read scaling — that needs exposed deferred reclamation, deliberately deferred; workloads needing per-key writes AND wait-free reads fall back to sharding's ~1.5-2x tax.

coverage.scenario: Bump arena (mass-free allocation for build-then-drop object graphs)
coverage.how: Existing arena<'r,T> + branded id<'r,T>: bump-class ~1-2 ns alloc, check-free deref by brand, cross-references as plain id fields with allocation-order locality; reset-reuse via the existing region staircase (fresh brand statically kills old ids). Matches bumpalo and beats safe-Rust checked-index arenas on deref.

coverage.scenario: Slab/pool with generational handles (stable identity + individual free)
coverage.how: Sealed pool: intrusive freelist reuse without re-zeroing, per-slot generation, get->option stale detection (never trap/alias) — slotmap-class ~2-5 ns insert/remove, 8-byte copy handles; also the safety keystone: detected-stale replaces well-typed UAF, so it strengthens T1 rather than taxing it.

coverage.scenario: Graphs (CFGs, dependency graphs, meshes) via index adjacency
coverage.how: Taught two-phase pattern over existing forms: build as vec-of-vecs keyed by u32/branded ids, freeze to CSR (two vecs); u32 ids halve traversal bytes; nothing new needed beyond ergonomic parallel arrays, which branded ids give.

coverage.scenario: Trees with parent pointers / cross-links (navigable node soups)
coverage.how: Taught pattern: nodes in pool (or arena for build-then-drop), parent/child/sibling as handle/id fields — the rowan/indextree shape; detach/reattach = 3-5 index stores; parent pointers are plain data, the exact safe-Rust production answer, within ~10-20% of pointer DOMs.

coverage.scenario: Intrusive lists / membership links (O(1) unlink from within the element)
coverage.how: Embedded index links inside pool elements: unlink = 2-4 stores to already-fetched lines + one base add, measured-parity class with list_del; multi-list membership = multiple link-pair fields. The irreducible residue (links through unowned free memory) exists only inside the sealed allocator, where it belongs.

coverage.scenario: Deque / FIFO work buffer (single-threaded)
coverage.how: Sealed deque as vec's ring sibling: masked power-of-two indices, push/pop ~2-3 ns, as_views two-slice access, one-memcpy growth — VecDeque-class by construction; chunked/linked fail shapes are not writable.

coverage.scenario: SPSC ring buffer (two-thread streaming handoff)
coverage.how: Sealed Lamport-refined ring (padded counters, cached opposing index, acquire/release only) with AFFINE endpoints: single-producer/single-consumer is a compile-time linearity fact, so the zero-RMW mechanism is retained safely — rtrb-class ~8-15 ns round trip, batched reserve/commit; also the minimal two-thread handoff the safety model types natively.

coverage.scenario: MPMC bounded queue (many-to-many channel buffer layer)
coverage.how: Sealed Vyukov queue (per-slot sequence numbers, one RMW per op, slot-local publication) with cloneable endpoints and move-through ownership; futex-parked blocking recv; Mutex<VecDeque>-class collapse is unwritable because that composition isn't taught and the sealed form is the one spelling.

coverage.scenario: Priority queue (heap) and the timer specialization
coverage.how: Checked-library implicit 4-ary heap over vec (pure index math, elidable checks, upper levels L1-resident) with taught lazy-deletion for decrease-key; timer wheel = taught composition (bucket array + embedded links + masked index) exactly as the map prescribes — O(1) insert/cancel at any count.

coverage.scenario: Work-stealing deque (per-worker task queue with thief end)
coverage.how: Sealed Chase-Lev inside the parallel runtime, with owner push/pop + steal also exposed as wsdeque ops for runtime authors; the SeqCst-fence proof is done once in TCB under model checking — the map's own recommendation (proved-once facade, never rewritten by ordinary writers).

coverage.scenario: Single/few-byte scanning (memchr class)
coverage.how: Checked-library kernels over the SIMD core: 32B compare + movemask + tzcnt, unrolled main loop, margin-covered tails, per-block bounds hoisted by requires facts; short-input path taught as a skeleton. Gated by experiment 2 (>=0.85x memchr crate).

coverage.scenario: Block-parallel validation and structural indexing (simdjson/simdutf class)
coverage.how: Same checked substrate: pshufb-as-table shuffle, clmul named intrinsic for quote masks, u64-bitmask-per-64B accumulation, zero data-dependent branches; taught as the block-classifier kernel skeleton (an existing pattern-family fit: branchless classifier P7 generalized to lanes).

coverage.scenario: Serialization/deserialization of structured messages
coverage.how: Three prescribed sub-shapes delivered: POD blit via layout views (memcpy-class); varint/TLV via checked cursor with one 8B unaligned load per field and per-message hoisted checks (upb/prost shape); offset-graph zero-copy via schema-instantiated validate-then-view; encode into vec<u8> with reserve (0-1 allocations/message).

coverage.scenario: Checksums and non-cryptographic hashing
coverage.how: Checked kernels over wide loads + widening mul + clmul + hardware crc32step with 3-stream interleave; short-key branch-minimal paths; both demand profiles (latency-at-16B, bulk GB/s) are pure register math with no ownership surface — the easiest checked-source par in the map.

coverage.scenario: Compression codec inner loops (LZ + entropy)
coverage.how: The two required sub-shapes are designed in: margin buffers legalize 32B wild copies without per-copy length loops, and copy_within with defined overlap semantics + multi-stream bit readers over padded input give the FSE/Huffman shape. But checked-source parity here is UNPROVEN and is the riskiest claim in the design — preregistered as experiment 3, with the fallback (sealed codec kernels) preregistered too. Until it passes, this row is not PAR.

coverage.scenario: Zero-copy typed views over binary layouts (packets, pages, records)
coverage.how: Schema-instantiated layout views: one upfront validation, then every field access is guaranteed single-load(+bswap) with an asm-diff parity gate vs C packed structs; variable-offset internals hand off to the checked cursor with once-proven facts — and unlike zerocopy/bytemuck, the validator is machine-generated, not trusted derive code.

coverage.scenario: General-purpose allocator fast path (mimalloc/jemalloc class)
coverage.how: n=1 sealed runtime allocator with mimalloc-class internals (TLS heaps, size-class freelists, batched remote frees, ~15-25 ns pair); all containers route through it invisibly. Honest residue stated: AUTHORING a new allocator in checked whitefoot is out of scope by design — its internals are the canonical type-changing-reuse shape and stay TCB.

coverage.scenario: Arena / bump allocation with scope-lifetime free
coverage.how: Same form as the F3 bump arena row: 2-4 instruction alloc, compiler-derived drops at region exit for drop-carrying T (mode a), reset-reuse via region staircase whose freshness proof is exactly the existing lexical-region discipline (mode b) — one catalog entry, two modes, as the map prescribes.

coverage.scenario: Slot pools / slabs: O(1) reuse without re-zeroing
coverage.how: Sealed pool recycles slots with no memset (TCB internals overwrite by construction; generations guard staleness): insert/remove ~2-5 ns, iteration at vec-class speed; the concurrent multi-producer variant is deliberately routed to sharded pools + channels (concurrency family), as the map suggests.

coverage.scenario: Uninitialized buffers: zero-init avoidance with tracked init extent
coverage.how: vec spare-capacity + the uninit-extent loan: sealed fillers (read/recv/decoder kernels) receive the uninitialized tail and len advances by the returned count; checked writers use cursor/push ops that maintain the extent by construction; the pass gate is codegen inspection showing zero memset in the read path (C parity). Field-wise struct init tracking is explicitly NOT included (smaller feature, deferred).

coverage.scenario: Memory-mapped data: file-backed byte regions
coverage.how: Read-only filemap + layout views covers LMDB-read/ripgrep/index workloads at par, with SIGBUS-on-truncation converted to a defined deterministic trap and an optional advisory-lock discipline. MISSING: shared-mutable MAP_SHARED (external-writer IPC) — it breaks single-process aliasing proofs and is deferred rather than faked; the map itself calls this split honest to bless separately or defer.

coverage.scenario: Portable SIMD vector types with runtime ISA dispatch
coverage.how: Language-primitive vector core + closed named-intrinsic escape + one-time dispatch attribute — the highway/std::simd recipe with the escape inside the same type system; the par bar (reproduce memchr/simdutf numbers when written in it) is exactly experiment 2.

coverage.scenario: Unaligned loads, endianness, and alignment control
coverage.how: load_le/be with guaranteed 1-mov(+bswap) lowering (asm parity gate), align(N) on types/allocations, padded<T> for false-sharing; the map's own n=1 verdict — one clean surface, no split.

coverage.scenario: Word-level bit operations: popcount/ctz/clz/pdep-pext and set-bit iteration
coverage.how: Fixed single-instruction method table + the taught x&=x-1 set-bit iteration idiom; pdep/pext carry a declared dispatch fallback for pre-Zen3 AMD; pure value ops, zero checker surface — the sealed map's probe loop and roaring-class structures bottom out here exactly.

coverage.scenario: Branchless selection and predication (cmov class)
coverage.how: select(c,a,b) with a perf tier (guaranteed cmov-class lowering, writer chooses per site) and a strict tier (verified never-branch for constant-time) — the two guarantee strengths the map asks for, one form; powers the checked sort/search kernels directly.

coverage.scenario: Software prefetch and non-temporal memory streams
coverage.how: prefetch(place) with vacuous semantics (never faults, exempt from validity obligations — checkable as a no-op) plus NT flags on bulk copy/fill; the compute-N-addresses-then-process batching idiom is expressible in ordinary loops since it is scheduling, not shape.

coverage.scenario: Thread lifecycle: spawn/join, worker pools, thread-local state
coverage.how: Region-scoped spawn (join-before-region-end reuses the lexical-region checker — the same proof shape as arena drop), sealed pools amortizing spawn, and per-worker env structs replacing writer-visible TLS (the profiler/allocator-grade static TLS lives inside TCB kernels where it is needed); borrowed-capture scoped spawn is exactly crossbeam::scope's contract, natively typed.

coverage.scenario: Atomic cells and the memory-ordering vocabulary
coverage.how: The closed trio (relaxed counter, acquire/release flag/once, publish via snapshot cell/channels/mutex) covers the ~95% stereotyped uses at hardware par; orderings are fixed per sealed form (so ARM pays no blanket SeqCst tax, x86 pays nothing); the C11 menu is absent BY DESIGN — implementer-grade needs are absorbed into project TCB, per the map's own analysis.

coverage.scenario: Mutual exclusion: mutex, rwlock, one-time init
coverage.how: Sealed word-sized futex adaptive mutex-protecting-data with affine guards (~10-20 ns uncontended), condvar, once; the read-mostly split is answered as the map prescribes: NO rwlock — the snapshot cell is the taught read-mostly form, so the reader-count plateau is unwritable.

coverage.scenario: Queues and channels: SPSC ring vs MPMC channel
coverage.how: Both shapes shipped sealed; the map's wished-for unification condition — 'a language with linear endpoint types could prove endpoint uniqueness' — is literally this design: affine endpoints make SPSC's zero-RMW mechanism a typed fact, and MPMC keeps Vyukov internals with parking-integrated blocking.

coverage.scenario: Concurrent keyed state: sharded maps and read-mostly tables
coverage.how: Sharded-lock map (checked composition) at dashmap-class for mixed loads; whole-table read-copy via snapshot cell at arc-swap-class (~2 ns reads); shard-by-thread composes from worker env structs + plain maps. MISSING: folly-CHM/papaya-class per-key-mutable wait-free-read tables (needs exposed reclamation) — same honest hole as the F2 concurrent-map row.

coverage.scenario: Safe memory reclamation (epoch/hazard/RCU class)
coverage.how: The map's 'strongest hide-it candidate' verdict is adopted wholesale: epoch reclamation lives invisibly inside the sealed snapshot cell and concurrent forms (pin ~2 ns class), consumers never see retire lists; the consequence — users cannot build NOVEL lock-free linked structures — is accepted and stated, with project-TCB extension as the路... the escalation path being a new sealed form plus hostile review (a catalog finding, not a user problem).

coverage.scenario: Data parallelism: parallel-for / fork-join with work stealing
coverage.how: Sealed rayon-shape runtime (Chase-Lev interior, geometric splitting, ~20-50 ns task overhead, bandwidth-bound ceiling) reaches rayon par; the WIN: parallel_reduce requires checked assoc/identity laws, so the optimizer may legally reassociate and vectorize reductions (including float and overflow-checked-int reductions) that C/C++/Rust compilers must refuse — the measured 3.3x checked-law reassociation result is exactly this mechanism, and disjoint-chunk mutation is proof-by-construction via consuming split_at.

coverage.scenario: Buffered sequential file I/O
coverage.how: Sealed buffered streams with region-tied fill_buf/consume (zero-copy in-place parsing, ripgrep-class), read_into vec spare capacity with zero memset (codegen-gated), explicit flush, plus the O_DIRECT aligned mode the database audience requires — both prescribed forms, one family.

coverage.scenario: Network sockets: TCP/UDP request paths
coverage.how: Sockets ride the completion ring (or classic nonblocking mode): vectored writes, multishot accept/recv, REUSEPORT sharding as a taught per-core pattern, steady-state allocation-free via moved-buffer recycling; UDP batching (recvmmsg/GSO) exposed as ring op variants; kernel-bypass (DPDK/XDP) excluded from the catalog as the map permits.

coverage.scenario: Readiness/completion multiplexing (epoll/kqueue/io_uring/IOCP class)
coverage.how: ONE completion-style sealed interface (submit ops, await completions, integrated timers/cancel) lowering to io_uring or epoll emulation — the tokio/libuv-proven portability layer; the safety crux the map names (buffer loaned to the kernel until completion) is a native affine move, nonforgeable by construction, which also unlocks registered fixed buffers by default.

coverage.scenario: mmap and shared memory
coverage.how: Read-only private maps: PAR via filemap + layout views + madvise/hugepage flags (LMDB-read/mold-input class), SIGBUS defined as a deterministic trap. MISSING: writable MAP_SHARED IPC and mmap-written outputs (mold-style) — external writers break the aliasing proof; deferred with an explicit escalation path (a future volatile-bytes sealed form) rather than a fake row; anonymous hugepage arenas hide inside the allocator.

coverage.scenario: Durability ordering: fsync discipline and write-ahead logging
coverage.how: The map's prescription implemented exactly: low-level layer (per-fd fdatasync, directory sync, atomic rename, O_DSYNC flag) as file-form ops, plus checked-library WAL (group commit, checksummed frames via the crc kernel) and atomic-file-replace recipes; group-commit batching is sequencing logic — pure checked source.

coverage.scenario: FFI call boundary (calling and being called by C)
coverage.how: Out-calls: PAR — bare ABI calls (~1-2 ns), repr(C) layouts, pointer+len from views, declarations confined to toolchain-gated human-approved trusted records with stated ownership contracts (the containment discipline the map demands). MISSING: C-calls-whitefoot entry points and long-lived callback registration — deferred; hot-loop out-call workloads (BLAS, codecs) are fully served, plugin-ABI hosting is not yet.

coverage.scenario: Process and OS services: spawn, time, entropy, signals
coverage.how: Sealed facade: posix_spawn-backed process spawn (~60 us regardless of parent RSS), vDSO clocks (~25 ns), buffered CSPRNG entropy (~2-5 ns), signals surfaced only as a shutdown flag/channel — all differentiating choices are implementation-internal, the map's clearest n=1 case.

tcb: Exact added trusted surface, in a ledger with a hard cap (~15k LOC of kernel code total, enumerated per kernel, no additions without owner-visible review): (1) runtime allocator (mimalloc-class, ~4-5k LOC) — the largest single item; (2) sequence-family kernel (vec/deque storage, growth, inline spill, slot moves, ~300 LOC); (3) map kernel (SwissTable control/slot coupling, rehash, entry-token internals, ~2k LOC); (4) omap kernel (B-tree nodes, ~1.5k LOC); (5) pool kernel (freelist slot reuse + generations, ~200 LOC); (6) concurrency kernels (futex mutex/once/condvar, SPSC ring, Vyukov MPMC, snapshot cell + epoch reclamation, Chase-Lev deque + scoped-thread runtime, ~3k LOC); (7) I/O layer (buffered/direct file, completion ring with io_uring/epoll lowerings, filemap, OS facade, ~4k LOC); (8) compiler-side trusted lowerings (SIMD op tables + dispatch, layout-view codegen, select/endian-load lowering guarantees) — these extend the existing compiler TCB and are gated by the existing codegen-parity + corpus infrastructure; (9) per-declaration extern-C trusted records (human-approved, toolchain-gated — the only per-instance trust, and it is the class the constitution already legitimizes). Verification story, per kernel class: hostile adversarial review BEFORE shipping (standing rule — every sealed op contract is a fact channel); concurrency kernels additionally get exhaustive small-bound model checking (all interleavings for <=4 threads x <=6 ops, loom/GenMC-style) plus per-architecture litmus suites (x86-TSO and ARM weak); data-structure kernels get differential fuzzing against behavioral oracles (hashbrown, BTreeMap, slotmap, VecDeque, crossbeam, rtrb, liburing) and sanitizer-instrumented isolation builds; every kernel gets a frozen conformance suite pinning its op contract; the DRF-by-construction theorem (no unsynchronized shared mutation is typeable, therefore sealed-form publication contracts compose to SC-for-DRF) gets a paper-grade review as the single most safety-critical claim. Nothing in the TCB is writer-suppliable: ordinary source cannot declare externs, replace the allocator, or open a sealed type.
checker_story: The checker gains six genuinely new decisions, all syntax-directed and deterministic, none requiring inference: (1) brand matching for id<'r,T> — syntactic equality of the region variable on the id's type against the arena's region, which the lexical-region machinery already tracks for slices; (2) compile-time constant arithmetic for margins and layouts — comparing declared constant offsets/widths against a constant M or layout size, the same constant-folding already required for array<T,N>; (3) layout-schema wellformedness — offsets/sizes/alignment are declared constants checked for overlap/bounds once per declaration, then field access is a table lookup; (4) the share/send judgment — a transitive structural bit per type computed exactly like a Copy bit (sendable: contains no borrow, which data already cannot; shareable-by-&: contains no sealed interior-mutable cell, a per-builtin flag), no flow analysis; (5) scoped spawn — reuses the existing lexical-region discipline verbatim: a spawned thread's obligations close at region end exactly as an arena's drop does, so no new escape analysis exists; (6) linear endpoints and I/O buffer loans — these are ordinary affine moves through the existing move rules; the checker does nothing new at all. Effect rows gain two labels (io, blocks) handled by the existing row machinery; requires-prologue elision extends unchanged to the new load ops (a bounds fact is a bounds fact). Sealed-form ops typecheck as ordinary function signatures — the checker enforces client obligations only and never looks inside a kernel. The reject-when-unsure posture is preserved everywhere: every new judgment is a structural yes/no over declared constants and type structure, so the checker remains frontend-simple with zero Rust-scale inference, and every rejection has a one-line reason naming the failed structural condition.
ai_writability: A mid-tier model can use this catalog from the spec alone because every scenario has exactly one taught spelling and misuse is either a compile error with a structural reason or a deterministic trap — never UB, never a silent perf cliff into an untaught shape (the fail shapes of the demand map — chained maps, linked deques, mutex-wrapped queues — are simply not in the catalog and mostly not writable). Form selection is a taught 3-question decision tree (lifetime shape: scoped-mass-free -> arena / churn-with-identity -> pool / sequence -> vec; lookup: unordered -> map / ranged -> omap; crossing threads: handoff -> channel / shared-read -> snapshot / shared-mutate -> mutex or shard). The historically hard parts of systems code are removed from the writer's plate entirely: there are no memory orderings to choose (the vocabulary is three fixed-ordering cells plus sealed forms), no lifetime puzzles in data (handles and ids are Copy data, borrows still cannot be stored), no closure capture semantics (env structs), and the concurrency-splitting proofs (endpoint uniqueness, buffer loans, disjoint chunks) fall out of affine moves the writer already performs. Declarative surfaces (layout schemas, extern records, dispatch attributes) are fill-in-the-table forms, the easiest genre for weak writers. The worst teaching hazard: SIMD kernel authorship — lane-width arithmetic, mask/tail handling, and dispatch-variant equivalence are genuinely subtle, and a weak writer can produce a correct-but-3x-slow kernel or a subtly wrong mask that is safe but wrong; mitigation is that F5 ships finished checked kernels (memchr/utf8/json/hash) so ordinary writers CALL rather than WRITE them, the block-classifier skeleton is taught as a fill-in pattern for new kernels, and wrongness is always a value bug caught by tests, never a memory-safety event. Secondary hazard: arena-vs-pool misselection (choosing never-freed arena for churny registries leaks; choosing pool for build-then-drop wastes generations) — a perf/memory finding, not a soundness one, and the decision tree's first question targets it directly.
risks: The DRF-by-construction theorem is the single point of catastrophic failure: one hole in the share judgment (e.g., a sealed form leaking an internal reference, or a Copy type smuggling a shareable cell) reinstates data races. Mitigation: paper-grade review + model checking, and the judgment's deliberate structural crudeness (reject-when-unsure).

Margin buffers are a new fact channel: 'writes past len are unobservable' must survive adversarial review, especially interaction with views, moves, and the optimizer's dead-store elimination; a leak of margin bytes into observable state would be an info-flow bug and a soundness embarrassment. Hostile review before shipping is mandatory (standing rule applies).

Codec parity (experiment 3) may fail, and with it the claim that F5 is fully checked-source; the preregistered fallback (sealed codec kernels) grows the TCB by the exact kind of large per-format trusted code the design tries to avoid.

Sealed-form op-contract creep: the entry-token API, cursor handoffs, and ring completion semantics are subtle contracts; each ambiguity discovered post-ship either breaks writers or grows the spec. The catalog appendix (op signatures + contracts) is itself ~4-6k tokens of taught material — the real D2 pressure is catalog size, not kernel rule count, and must be token-budgeted and measured.

TCB growth pressure is structural: every future par gap will tempt a new sealed kernel (the papaya-class map, shared-mutable mmap, inbound FFI are already queued); without the ledger cap and owner-visible review, the 'no unsafe escape' claim erodes into 'unsafe lives in an ever-growing blessed basement'.

Closing user-space lock-free authorship (no exposed reclamation, no raw atomics) bets that the sealed concurrent catalog is complete enough; a systems audience that needs one novel lock-free structure the project won't ship walks away — the escalation path (project-authored sealed form per request) has real latency.

Checked-source SIMD kernels must be re-proven at par per ISA (AVX-512, SVE, NEON quirks like missing movemask); dispatch-table and lowering-guarantee maintenance is permanent compiler-TCB work, and a regression there silently un-pars all of F5.

The one-probe entry API without closures (affine vacancy token) is novel; if the token's region discipline proves too restrictive in real writer code (e.g., needing map growth between entry() and insert_at()), the fallback is two probes — a measurable get-or-insert regression on counter/cache workloads.

Composition patterns (LRU, interner, timer wheel, sharded map) are claimed at par via composition tests that have not run; a miss (e.g., find_by env-comparator overhead breaking interning's 2x bar) converts a REJECT row into a needed capability late.
first_experiments: EXP-1 (sealed associative par): implement the sealed map kernel (SwissTable internals, affine-token entry API) plus the checked foldhash-class default hasher; run the locked workload — 1M uniform-random u64->u64, DRAM-resident, measuring lookup-hit ns/op, insert-with-growth amortized ns/op, and a get-or-insert counter loop via the entry token — against hashbrown 0.15+foldhash on the same host, same allocator class. PASS: all three within 1.10x of hashbrown. FAIL: any metric >1.25x, or the entry-token API cannot express one-probe get-or-insert (design fault, not tuning fault). Between bands: one rerun after fixing identified mechanical issues, then verdict. No tuning of the protocol from its result.

EXP-2 (checked-source SIMD par): write memchr (find-first, rare-needle) as CHECKED whitefoot source over the SIMD core: dispatch(avx2) kernel, 32B compares + movemask + tzcnt, margin-covered tail, requires-prologue hoisting the per-block bounds checks; verify by report that zero bounds checks remain in the main loop, then benchmark 16KB and 1MB random haystacks plus <=16B short inputs against memchr crate 2.7. PASS: >=0.85x memchr's GB/s on both long-haystack sizes AND <=2.0x its ns on short inputs AND the elision report confirms all main-loop checks discharged (not weakened — discharged). FAIL: <0.70x bulk throughput, or any main-loop check surviving undischarged while claiming the number.

EXP-3 (margin-buffer codec bet — the riskiest delta): implement an LZ4-block decompressor as checked source: output vec<u8> with margin 32, wild copies as margined 32B vector stores, overlapping matches via the defined copy_within, input as validated margin bytes with amortized cursor checks; benchmark single-core GB/s on the Silesia corpus against LZ4 1.9 reference decode, with malformed-input fuzzing (must error, never trap mid-buffer into corrupt state, never expose margin bytes). PASS: >=0.75x LZ4 1.9 GB/s with all fuzz properties holding. FAIL: <0.60x, or any fuzz property violation — preregistered consequence of FAIL: the margin delta is retained only if EXP-2 needed it for tails, and codecs move to sealed TCB kernels; the coverage row stays PARTIAL and says so.
